title: Epipactis spiralis
intro: Epipactis spiralis, also known as the spiral helleborine, is a type of orchid that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including bronchitis, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The bioactive constituents of Epipactis spiralis include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Epipactis spiralis is often prepared as a tincture, decoction, or infusions to be used in the treatment of respiratory issues, and it is also sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
taxonomy: Epipactis spiralis belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a species of terrestrial orchid. It is a member of the genus Epipactis and is commonly known as the narrow-leaved helleborine. The classification of Epipactis spiralis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Angiospermophyta, Class: Monocotyledonae, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Genus: Epipactis, Species: E. spiralis. Epipactis spiralis is native to Europe and parts of Western Asia. This plant is also known as the narrow-leaved helleborine due to its narrow, spiraling leaves.

properties: Epipactis spiralis, also known as the fairy orchid, is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
